name: Keep Configurable Data at High Levels
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 17: Smells and Heuristics (G35)
problem: Magic numbers and default values buried deep in low-level implementation code are invisible to the caller, cannot be overridden without modifying internals, and are easily duplicated inconsistently across the codebase.
steps: Identify every literal constant or default value that represents a business rule or configuration choice.
Move it to the highest abstraction level that uses it: a top-level constant, config file, or environment variable.
Name it clearly so its intent is obvious without a comment.
Pass the value down to lower-level code through constructors, method parameters, or a config object.
Never let low-level code define values that higher-level code should control.
